title: RNNs, LSTMs & GRUs (Historical Context)
summary: Sequential processing: hidden state $h_t$ updated at each timestep. LSTMs and GRUs add gating to control information flow. Transformers replaced them because: sequential = slow (no parallelism), vanishing gradients over long sequences, and attention directly accesses any position.
key_insights: RNN: $h_t = \sigma(W_h h_{t-1} + W_x x_t + b)$. Hidden state carries information from all past inputs. Sequential — can't parallelize.
Vanishing gradients: $\partial h_T / \partial h_1 = \prod_{t=1}^{T-1} W_h \sigma'$. If $\|W_h\| < 1$: vanishes exponentially. Can't learn long-range dependencies.
LSTM: adds cell state $c_t$ (the 'memory highway') + forget/input/output gates (sigmoid → 0 or 1). Gates decide what to remember/forget.
GRU: simplified LSTM. Two gates instead of three (reset, update). Fewer params, similar performance.

Why transformers won: (1) Parallel — all positions computed simultaneously. (2) Direct access — attention connects any two positions in $O(1)$. (3) No information bottleneck — no fixed-size hidden state.
RNNs survive in niche: online/streaming processing where you can't wait for the full sequence, very long sequences where $O(n^2)$ attention is prohibitive.

mathematical_core.lstm_forget: $f_t = \sigma(W_f [h_{t-1}, x_t] + b_f)$ — what to forget from cell state
mathematical_core.lstm_input: $i_t = \sigma(W_i [h_{t-1}, x_t] + b_i)$ — what new info to store
mathematical_core.gru: $z_t = \sigma(W_z [h_{t-1}, x_t])$, $r_t = \sigma(W_r [h_{t-1}, x_t])$, $h_t = (1-z_t) \odot h_{t-1} + z_t \odot \tanh(W [r_t \odot h_{t-1}, x_t])$

derivations.title: Why LSTM gates use sigmoid — connection to FOUNDATIONS.ACTIVATIONS

steps.name: Gate values must be in $[0, 1]$
steps.math: Forget gate $f_t$: 0 = forget completely, 1 = remember perfectly. Sigmoid outputs $\in (0, 1)$ naturally.

steps.name: Candidate values use tanh
steps.math: New cell content $\tilde{c}_t = \tanh(\cdot)$ outputs $\in (-1, 1)$. Centered at zero — balanced positive/negative updates.

steps.name: Cell state as residual highway
steps.math: $c_t = f_t \odot c_{t-1} + i_t \odot \tilde{c}_t$. When $f_t \approx 1, i_t \approx 0$: $c_t \approx c_{t-1}$. Perfect gradient flow — same principle as transformer residuals.

derivations.consequence: LSTM cell state = selective residual connection. Forget gate controls how much past to keep. This is the historical precursor to transformer skip connections.

related: TRANSFORMER.SELF_ATTENTION — replaced RNNs by solving the same problems differently
TRANSFORMER.RESIDUALS — cell state = early residual highway

notes: Understanding RNN limitations is important context for WHY transformers were designed the way they are. The vanishing gradient → LSTM gate → residual connection → transformer evolution.
